title: Living in Boulder vlog 2 - 18 - 2017: Bike Racer Levels Pages

enrichment.summary: Kyle vlogs from Boulder about his ongoing work on a 3D model of the Circus Maximus in SketchUp/Google Earth, including building a flat floor, modeling Roman race horses and chariots, and calculating stadium capacity. He also describes starting a 'bike racer levels page' for his website, which overlays modern competitive cycling distance records onto the Circus Maximus model to help visualize the ancient races.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Excited by the project; completing the flat floor made the building suddenly feel simple and cohesive
Wants to contextualize ancient chariot racing distances using modern cycling records to make the scale relatable
Loves having very large pages (3,000+ entries) on his website despite the performance problems they cause
Appreciates his fast computer even after three years, though Google Earth still pushes it to its limits

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I haven't done a video in 6 days because I was working on the theater of Kisestra.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I wanted to distinguish the powertime hell and build all the stairs up to it to make it seem smaller in a cohesive area.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Using the polygon tool in SketchUp, I realized the powertime hell became very simple to make in a business confusing building.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I decided to work on the circus maximus some more but realized I didn't have all the data to calculate the exact capacity.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I built a 3D race horse and a four-horse chariot, totaling 48 horses and 12 guys.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I wanted to make the circus maximus easy for people nowadays, so I focused on the distances they raced.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I started working on my bike race level page on my website to overlay the circus maximus with bike races.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I imagined a movie set in an alternate parallel reality where Romans race bikes.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to include track records of all bike distances and tracks from all countries on my website.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm currently working on flying 200 and 500 distances and will fix them on my website with time travel age.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I will eventually plug the circus maximus into Boulder but won't manually place people there since I already did it for the circus maximus.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I was listening to something cool but can't remember what it was.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: It's awesome to work on my website with a fast computer, even though it's three years old.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Most of the work I've done on Google Earth has been compacted, and I wonder how much faster a new computer would be.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My computer struggles with my website because it has 3,000 pages, and fixing broken links takes hours.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I love having a big website, but the downside is that I rarely look at it myself.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The problem with a big website is that you don't actually spend much time looking at it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm almost done with the 250-page project on my website.
